title.purpose: The editable project name in the top bar. The single largest text element in the app.

body.purpose: Field values, checklist titles, property lists, general UI content.

label.purpose: Captions, tags, badges, field hints. Always Graphite or Fog, never Ink.

shadows.name: tight
shadows.purpose: The floating dimension-label chip over the plan/elevation canvas.

shadows.name: panel
shadows.purpose: The project-picker dropdown menu — the app's one true floating overlay.

components.name: Icon Button
components.description: 36px (32px compact) square button for toolbar actions. No color until interaction.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-icon" aria-label="Action"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"><path d="M12 5v14M5 12h14"/></svg></button>
components.css: .ds-btn-icon { display: grid; place-items: center; width: 36px; height: 36px; border: 1px solid #d0d5d8; border-radius: 6px; background: #ffffff; color: #0c0e0f; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s; } .ds-btn-icon:hover { background: #f4f7f9; } .ds-btn-icon:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #00767e; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Tab Button (active)
components.description: View-switcher tab (Plan / Elevation / Data). Active state is a border + fill, not a solid color block.
components.css: .ds-tab { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; min-height: 34px; padding: 0 10px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 6px; background: transparent; color: #4a4e50; font-size: 0.86rem; font-weight: 680; cursor: pointer; } .ds-tab:hover { background: #f4f7f9; } .ds-tab-active { border-color: #d0d5d8; background: #f4f7f9; color: #0c0e0f; }

components.name: Selection State (Wall / Row)
components.description: The Tinted Selection Rule: every selected/active row or handle in the app uses this exact border + soft-fill pairing, never a solid fill.
components.css: .ds-row { display: flex; align-items: center; width: 100%; min-height: 42px; padding: 8px 10px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 6px; background: transparent; text-align: left; cursor: pointer; color: #0c0e0f; font-size: 0.84rem; } .ds-row:hover { background: #f4f7f9; } .ds-row-selected { border-color: #04585c; background: #d6edee; }

components.name: Uncertainty Badge
components.description: Signature component. Approximate data reads Caution Amber, unknown/invalid data reads Alarm Red; a fully-known value shows no badge at all.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 9px; border-radius: 999px; font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 650; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-badge-approx { background: #fde8cb; color: #8d5500; } .ds-badge-unknown { background: #fce3e1; color: #be3029; }

components.name: Field Input
components.description: Standard text/number field. Hairline border at rest, Fog on hover, Petrol focus ring, Alarm Red when invalid.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; min-height: 38px; padding: 8px 10px; border: 1px solid #d0d5d8; border-radius: 6px; background: #ffffff; color: #0c0e0f; font-size: 0.86rem; } .ds-input:hover { border-color: #6e7276; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #00767e; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-input[aria-invalid='true'] { border-color: #be3029; }

components.name: Warning Panel
components.description: Advisory panel using the Caution pair — distinct from both ordinary flat panels and genuine Alarm Red errors.
components.html:
Check placement

This wall's height is approximate.
components.css: .ds-warning { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #d9ad57; border-radius: 8px; background: #fde8cb; color: #8d5500; } .ds-warning h3 { margin: 0; color: #0c0e0f; font-size: 0.9rem; } .ds-warning p { margin: 0; font-size: 0.8rem; }

components.name: Brand Mark
components.description: Signature component. The only place Oxblood appears anywhere in the interface.
components.css: .ds-brand-mark { display: grid; place-items: center; width: 38px; height: 38px; border-radius: 8px; background: #5b1622; color: #ffffff; font-weight: 750; }

narrative.overview: Sightlines should feel like a clean exhibition drafting table: a white working surface where wall plans, object checklists, measurements, and placement decisions can sit together without visual noise. The interface is minimalist but not generic, precise but not cold, elegant without becoming decorative. Black linework, crisp typography, measured spacing, subtle grids, and restrained color make the workspace feel trustworthy, calm, and professional — a serious studio instrument, not a CAD program, not a SketchUp clone, not a generic SaaS dashboard, and not a collections-management database. Color is functional before it is expressive. The surface is white; text is black and grey; a single petrol blue-green carries every routine interactive state (selection, focus, in-progress); a deep oxblood appears exactly once, on the brand mark, as the one deliberate flourish the system allows itself. A separate amber-caution color exists purely to make uncertain data visible without making it feel like an error — a distinct concern from both the interactive color and the alarm-red used for genuine invalid states.
keyCharacteristics: Pure white canvas; near-black ink text; barely-tinted cool greys for panel separation
One interactive hue (petrol) governs selection, focus, and active state everywhere
Oxblood is rationed to a single component: the brand mark
Selection is always a border + soft tint, never a solid filled button
Uncertainty has its own amber-caution language, independent of both petrol and danger-red
Flat by default; shadow appears only on the two genuinely floating elements in the whole app

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Petrol is the only color used for routine interaction anywhere in the app.

rules.name: The Rationed Accent Rule
rules.body: Oxblood appears in exactly one place: the brand mark.

rules.name: The Tinted Selection Rule
rules.body: No component uses a solid filled background for its default or selected state; selection is always a border plus a soft-tint wash.

rules.name: The Caution Ladder Rule
rules.body: Confidence in data has exactly two visual tiers — Caution Amber for approximate, Alarm Red for unknown/invalid — both independent of Petrol.

rules.name: The Fractional Weight Rule
rules.body: Type weights are tuned per element along Inter's variable axis (650/680/700/720/750/760) rather than snapped to standard steps.

rules.name: The Floating-Only Shadow Rule
rules.body: Shadow exclusively marks elements floating above the page (the dropdown menu, the dimension-label chip); everything else is flat with a hairline border.

dos: Do keep Petrol as the only color used for selection, focus, and active state.
Do express selection as a border + soft-tint background, never a solid fill.
Do keep the caution/danger pair independent of the interactive color family.
Do default every panel to flat + hairline border; reserve shadow for the dropdown menu and the floating dimension label only.
Do tune type weight in small increments rather than jumping between a few standard steps.
donts: Don't introduce a second decorative accent color — Oxblood is rationed to the brand mark.
Don't fill a button with a solid brand color; this system has no filled-primary-button anywhere.
Don't let this look like a CAD program, a SketchUp clone, a generic SaaS dashboard, or a collections-management database.
Don't reuse Caution Amber or Alarm Red for anything other than data-confidence and error states.
Don't add shadow to a panel that lives in normal document flow.
